Details: Three studies were found that included alternative processes for the treatment of arsenic, including electroadsorption [2692], distillation [2702], and electrochemical deposition [2708]. The electroadsorption study utilized GAC connected to either an anode or a cathode in a filter-press cell. Both scenarios proved to poorly remove arsenic, citing up to 66 and up to 50 percent removal, respectively. The point of use distillation study was found to be successful even without preoxidation, reporting greater than 99 percent removal of arsenic across three trials. Electrochemical deposition onto GAC was also found to be effective, removing up to greater than 99 percent arsenic. All three alternative treatments were studied at the bench-scale. Further research is needed to assess the feasibility of application of each process at full-scale.
